images.alt: Dashboard titled “2023 number of state submitted actions” displays data visualizations for SPAs, 1915(c) amendments, 1915(c) new or renewals, and 1915(b) actions, broken down by quarter. The total number of all state-submitted actions is 1,924. For SPAs, the total is 1,414, with 1,297 standard and 117 disaster relief submissions. SPA submissions by quarter show Q1 with 327 standard and 44 disaster relief, Q2 with 267 and 72, Q3 with 411 and 1, and Q4 with 292 standard submissions only. For 1915(c) amendments, the yearly total is 408, split into 267 standard and 141 Appendix K. For 1915(c) amendments, the yearly total is 408, split into 267 standard and 141 Appendix K. Submissions by quarter show Q1 with 34 standard and 35 Appendix K, Q2 with 60 and 73, Q3 with 83 and 22, and Q4 with 80 standard and 11 Appendix K. The 1915(c) new or renewals totaled 61 for the year, with 29 in Q1, 11 in Q2, 12 in Q3, and 9 in Q4. The 1915(b) actions reached a yearly total of 41, with 9 in Q1, 8 in Q2, 14 in Q3, and 10 in Q4.

images.alt: Dashboard titled “2023 number of approved actions” shows four bar charts summarizing approvals by quarter across different categories, with a total of 1,815 approved state actions. For SPAs, the total is 1,324, with 1,165 standard and 159 disaster relief approvals. Approvals by quarter include Q1 with 218 standard and 49 disaster relief, Q2 with 343 and 88, Q3 with 235 and 17, and Q4 with 369 standard and 5 disaster relief. For 1915(c) amendments, the yearly total is 376, divided into 226 standard and 150 Appendix K approvals. Approvals by quarter show Q1 with 40 standard and 57 Appendix K, Q2 with 59 and 49, Q3 with 50 and 38, and Q4 with 77 standard and 6 Appendix K. The 1915(c) new/renewals category totals 73 approvals, with 11 in Q1, 36 in Q2, 19 in Q3, and 7 in Q4. For 1915(b) actions, the yearly total is 42, with 10 in Q1, 10 in Q2, 7 in Q3, and 15 in Q4.

images.alt: Dashboard titled “2023 number of approved actions and percentage approved on first clock” includes a total of 1,525 state actions. For SPAs, the yearly total is 1,093. In Q1, there were 165 standard and 41 disaster relief approvals; in Q2, 285 standard and 74 disaster relief; in Q3, 200 standard and 11 disaster relief; and in Q4, 306 standard and 11 disaster relief approvals. The yearly percentage average for SPAs is 83%, with Q1 showing 82% standard and 85% disaster relief, Q2 showing 86% and 84%, Q3 showing 87% and 73%, and Q4 showing 89% for standard and 40% for disaster relief. For 1915(c) amendments, the yearly total is 351. Q1 had 40 standard and 54 Appendix K approvals, Q2 had 59 and 49, Q3 had 50 and 38, and Q4 had 58 standard and 3 Appendix K. The yearly percentage average for 1915(c) amendments is 93%, with Q1 showing 95% standard and 93% Appendix K, Q2 showing 97% and 94%, Q3 showing 94% and 92%, and Q4 showing 98% standard and 100% Appendix K. For 1915(c) new or renewals, the total is 44, with 5 in Q1, 26 in Q2, 10 in Q3, and 3 in Q4. The yearly percentage average is 60%, with Q1 at 40%, Q2 at 82%, Q3 at 21%, and Q4 at 43%. For 1915(b) actions, the yearly total is 37, with 9 in Q1, 8 in Q2, 6 in Q3, and 14 in Q4. The yearly percentage average is 88%, with Q1 at 89%, Q2 at 88%, Q3 at 87%, and Q4 at 87%.

images.alt: Dashboard Title: 2022 Number Approved & Percentage Approved on First Clock. The total number of approved state actions in 2022 was 1,465. For SPAs, the yearly total was 980, with Q1 including 190 standard and 5 disaster relief, Q2 with 237 and 8, Q3 with 194 and 10, and Q4 with 326 standard and 10 disaster relief. The yearly percentage average for SPAs was 82%, with standard approvals ranging from 77% to 89% and disaster relief ranging from 60% to 78% across quarters. For 1915(c) amendments, the yearly total was 442, with Q1 including 34 standard and 144 Appendix K, Q2 with 38 and 52, Q3 with 27 and 88, and Q4 with 47 standard and 12 Appendix K. The yearly percentage average was 92%, with standard consistently at or near 100%, and Appendix K ranging from 60% to 95%. For 1915(c) new or renewals, the total was 25, with 4 in Q1, 13 in Q2, 5 in Q3, and 3 in Q4. The percentage approved on first clock for this group averaged 50%, with Q1 at 33%, Q2 at 77%, Q3 at 40%, and Q4 at 33%. For 1915(b) actions, the total was 18, including 3 in Q1, 6 in Q2, 4 in Q3, and 5 in Q4. The yearly percentage average was 56%, with quarterly values ranging from 40% to 75%.
images.altReason: alt text is 1165 characters — consider a caption or description

images.alt: Dashboard Title: 2022 Median Processing Days from Submission to Approval. The yearly median processing time for SPAs was 76 days. In Q1, standard SPAs took 71 days and disaster relief took 63; in Q2, standard took 73 and disaster relief 78; in Q3, standard took 81 and disaster relief 75; and in Q4, standard took 75 days while disaster relief took 67. For 1915(c) amendments, the overall median was 54 days. Q1 had 75 days for standard and 40 for Appendix K; Q2 had 86 and 44; Q3 had 82 and 42; and Q4 had 82 for standard and 43 for Appendix K. The median processing time for 1915(c) new or renewals was 126 days, with Q1 at 160, Q2 at 89, Q3 at 161, and Q4 at 104. For 1915(b) actions, the median was 89 days, with Q1 at 72, Q2 at 90, Q3 at 132, and Q4 at 286.
